name: Lepe - Cartaya via the Litoral Greenway
name_original: Lepe - Cartaya por la Vía Verde del Litoral
region_intro: Hiking around Lepe, located in the province of Huelva, Spain, offers exploration of diverse natural landscapes. The region is characterized by extensive wetlands, estuaries where the Odiel and Tinto rivers meet, lagoons, and salt flats, all contributing to a rich ecosystem. Terrain is generally flat, providing accessible paths through coastal areas and natural parks. These features support a variety of flora and fauna, making it a notable area for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Lepe The most popular hiking route is West Coast of Huelva Trail, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the wetlands and estuary. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lepe - Cartaya via the Litoral Greenway, a difficult 12.1 miles (19.4 km) path. This trail connects towns along a greenway, providing a longer distance option. Local hikers also love the La Antilla Beach – La Antilla Seafront Promenade loop from La Antilla, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Lepe is defined by coastal paths, extensive wetlands, and estuarine environments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Lepe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Lepe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lepe?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes around Lepe, offering a variety of experiences. These include 13 easy, 12 moderate, and 5 difficult trails, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Lepe?
A: The terrain around Lepe is generally flat, characterized by coastal paths, extensive wetlands, and estuarine environments where the Odiel and Tinto rivers meet. This makes for accessible paths through diverse natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Lepe?
A: Yes, Lepe offers several easy and family-friendly hiking options. Many paths within the Marismas del Odiel Natural Park are easily accessible due to the generally flat terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonita Beach – Bonita Beach loop from La Antilla&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.6 km route.

Q: What natural features or wildlife can I see while hiking in Lepe?
A: Lepe, particularly within the Marismas del Odiel Natural Park, is an ornithological paradise. You can spot numerous migratory birds like flamingos, spoonbills, cormorants, and herons. The diverse ecosystems also include lagoons, salt flats, and beaches. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3970452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Marshes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes and Saltworks of Isla Cristina&lt;/a&gt; for excellent wildlife viewing.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Lepe?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17221141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Antilla Beach – La Antilla Seafront Promenade loop from La Antilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 9.1 km trail through coastal areas. Another easy circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonita Beach – Bonita Beach loop from La Antilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the Marismas del Odiel, you can explore several interesting spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4695054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Litoral Greenway&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic path connecting towns. You might also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Zúñiga&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/982208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Portil Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lepe?
A: The Marismas del Odiel Natural Park is a crucial stop for migratory birds, making autumn and spring excellent seasons for birdwatching and pleasant hiking temperatures. The generally flat and well-ventilated paths are enjoyable year-round, but avoiding the peak heat of summer might be preferable for longer hikes.

Q: How accessible are the trails in Lepe by public transport?
A: While specific public transport details for each trailhead are not provided, the region is generally accessible. The Marismas del Odiel Natural Park has established trails, some departing from visitor centers, which often have better public transport connections or parking facilities. It's advisable to check local transport options for specific starting points.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lepe?
A: The hiking routes in Lepe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the extensive wetlands, and the accessible paths that offer opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially birds.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in Lepe?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983835873/lepe-cartaya-via-the-litoral-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lepe - Cartaya via the Litoral Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.4 km path that connects towns, providing a substantial distance option through the region's unique environment.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Lepe?
A: While many natural parks in Spain have regulations regarding dogs, especially in protected areas like the Marismas del Odiel Natural Park, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife. Always check specific trail regulations or visitor center guidelines before heading out with your pet.
tips: The Vía Verde itinerary has a total length of 48 km and connects Huelva with Ayamonte using the route of the old railway line. This same railway line was formerly used to transport fish between Isla Cristina and Lepe to later be sent to Madrid, as well as minerals from El Andévalo bound for the coast.

Built in the 15th century by Don Pedro Zúñiga, it was conceived to defend and monitor the land in the area that was regularly lashed by pirates. In the 16th century it was reinforced by adding defense elements and towards the end of the 17th century it was a refuge for citizens from Portuguese attacks. Its history as a fortress came to an end in 1812 and at some point it was planned to be demolished. Luckily today it is in perfect condition and has been declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1985.
Beautiful square in the centre of Cartaya, where we find the municipal building (affectionately called the “White House”), a modern-designed music hall and the main church of the city.
